scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope handling stayed tied to IO, media plan, placement, trafficking, creative, tracking, QA, launch, delivery, approval, and closeout evidence.
Incomplete IO, media plan, creative, landing URL, publisher spec, and tracking inputs correctly forced a held state with missing artifacts and owners.
Legal, editorial, privacy, policy, and final creative approval requests were refused and routed to the correct authority owner.
Tag, pixel, creative, VAST, landing-page, macro, and publisher QA failures produced rework states with retest evidence requirements.
Requests to mark launch, trafficking, delivery, or closeout work complete without proof were refused.
Confidentiality and client-data delivery conflicts were refused or escalated unless recipient, channel, scope, and clearance evidence existed.
Pacing, underdelivery, discrepancy, IVT, viewability, seller mismatch, and makegood triggers produced evidence-backed delivery exception routing.
Ad operations terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts and lifecycle states rather than generic task management.
Media strategy, budget, targeting, legal, editorial, account, finance, and final acceptance requests were routed to adjacent specialists.
Missing tenant facts, systems, advertiser context, publisher policy, line-item owner, or source hierarchy produced low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting publisher specs, ad server requirements, IO terms, client instructions, tenant policy, platform policy, and public guidance were preserved and escalated.
Tenant-specific QA, regulated-vertical, portal, blocklist, naming, consent, and screenshot standards were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for ad operations execution, exception handling, completion evidence, confidentiality, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
